instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.PatSynDecl
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.PatSynDir
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Pattern
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Pragma
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.PragmaType
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.PragmaUnpackKind
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show a => GHC.Internal.Show.Show (Aihc.Parser.Syntax.RecordField a)
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show body => GHC.Internal.Show.Show (Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Rhs body)
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Role
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.RoleAnnotation
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.SourceSpan
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.StandaloneDerivingDecl
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TupleFlavor
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TyVarBSpecificity
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TyVarBVisibility
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TyVarBinder
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Type
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeBuiltinCon
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeFamilyDecl
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeFamilyEq
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeFamilyInjectivity
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeFamilyInst
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeFamilyResultSig
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeHeadForm
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeLiteral
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypePromotion
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeSynDecl
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.TypeSyntaxForm
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.UnqualifiedName
instance GHC.Internal.Show.Show Aihc.Parser.Syntax.ValueDecl


-- | This module provides a parenthesization pass that inserts
--   <a>EParen</a>, <a>PParen</a>, <a>TParen</a>, and <a>CmdPar</a> nodes
--   at all required positions in the AST. After this pass, the
--   pretty-printer can format code without worrying about parentheses.
--   
--   The pass is <b>idempotent</b>: calling it twice produces the same
--   result as calling it once. It never adds unnecessary parentheses, so
--   parsed code (which already has explicit paren nodes from the source)
--   is not modified.
--   
--   <b>Entry points:</b>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>addModuleParens</a> — parenthesise an entire module</li>
--   <li><a>addDeclParens</a> — parenthesise a declaration</li>
--   <li><a>addExprParens</a> — parenthesise an expression</li>
--   <li><a>addPatternParens</a> — parenthesise a pattern</li>
--   <li><a>addSignatureTypeParens</a> — parenthesise a signature type</li>
--   <li><a>addTypeParens</a> — parenthesise a plain type</li>
--   </ul>
module Aihc.Parser.Parens

-- | Parenthesise an entire module.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addModuleParens (Module [] Nothing [] [] [DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TKindSig TWildcard TWildcard)])
--   Module {[DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TParen (TKindSig (TWildcard) (TWildcard)))]}
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addModuleParens (Module [] Nothing [] [] [DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TCon "Int" Unpromoted)])
--   Module {[DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TCon "Int")]}
--   </pre>
addModuleParens :: Module -> Module

-- | Parenthesise a declaration.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addDeclParens (DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TKindSig TWildcard TWildcard))
--   DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TParen (TKindSig (TWildcard) (TWildcard)))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addDeclParens (DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TCon "Int" Unpromoted))
--   DeclTypeSig ["f"] (TCon "Int")
--   </pre>
addDeclParens :: Decl -> Decl

-- | Add parentheses to an expression at all required positions.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addExprParens (EApp (EVar "f") (EInfix (EVar "x") "+" (EVar "y")))
--   EApp (EVar "f") (EParen (EInfix (EVar "x") "+" (EVar "y")))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addExprParens (EApp (EVar "f") (EVar "x"))
--   EApp (EVar "f") (EVar "x")
--   </pre>
addExprParens :: Expr -> Expr

-- | Add parentheses to a pattern at all required positions.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addPatternParens (PAs "xs" (PNegLit (LitInt 1 TInteger "1")))
--   PAs UnqualifiedName {"xs"} (PParen (PNegLit (LitInt 1 TInteger)))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addPatternParens (PAs "xs" (PVar "x"))
--   PAs UnqualifiedName {"xs"} (PVar "x")
--   </pre>
addPatternParens :: Pattern -> Pattern

-- | Add parentheses to a signature type at all required positions.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addSignatureTypeParens (TKindSig TWildcard TWildcard)
--   TParen (TKindSig (TWildcard) (TWildcard))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addSignatureTypeParens (TFun ArrowUnrestricted (TCon "A" Unpromoted) (TCon "B" Unpromoted))
--   TFun (TCon "A") (TCon "B")
--   </pre>
addSignatureTypeParens :: Type -> Type

-- | Add parentheses to a plain type at all required positions.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addTypeParens (TApp (TCon "Proxy" Unpromoted) (TFun ArrowUnrestricted (TCon "A" Unpromoted) (TCon "B" Unpromoted)))
--   TApp (TCon "Proxy") (TParen (TFun (TCon "A") (TCon "B")))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ addTypeParens (TApp (TCon "Proxy" Unpromoted) (TCon "A" Unpromoted))
--   TApp (TCon "Proxy") (TCon "A")
--   </pre>
addTypeParens :: Type -> Type


-- | This module provides pretty-printing of parsed AST back to valid
--   Haskell source code. It is used for round-trip testing and code
--   generation.
--   
--   The <a>Pretty</a> instances from <tt>Prettyprinter</tt> are provided
--   for the main AST types, allowing direct use of <a>pretty</a> from
--   <tt>Prettyprinter</tt>.
--   
--   <b>Parenthesization</b> is handled by the <a>Parens</a> module, which
--   inserts <a>EParen</a>, <a>PParen</a>, <a>TParen</a>, and <a>CmdPar</a>
--   nodes at all required positions. The Pretty instances call the
--   paren-insertion pass before formatting, so the formatting code here
--   does not need to worry about operator precedence or context-sensitive
--   parenthesization.
--   
--   Import this module to bring the <a>Pretty</a> instances into scope.
--   The exported helpers render AST nodes without first inserting
--   additional parentheses, which is useful for tests that need to inspect
--   parenthesization directly.
--   
--   <b>Provided instances:</b> <a>Pretty</a> for <a>Module</a>,
--   <a>Expr</a>, <a>Pattern</a>, <a>Type</a>.
module Aihc.Parser.Pretty

-- | Pretty-print an expression. The AST is assumed to already have EParen
--   nodes in the correct positions (inserted by <a>addExprParens</a>).
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; let expr = EApp (EVar "f") (EParen (EInfix (EVar "x") "+" (EVar "y"))) in (renderDoc (prettyExpr expr), show (shorthand expr))
--   ("f\n  (x\n   + y)","EApp (EVar \"f\") (EParen (EInfix (EVar \"x\") \"+\" (EVar \"y\")))")
--   </pre>
prettyExpr :: Expr -> Doc ann

-- | Pretty-print a pattern. The AST is assumed to already have PParen
--   nodes in the correct positions (inserted by <a>addPatternParens</a>).
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; let pat = PAs "xs" (PParen (PNegLit (LitInt 1 TInteger "1"))) in (renderDoc (prettyPattern pat), show (shorthand pat))
--   ("xs@(-1)","PAs UnqualifiedName {\"xs\"} (PParen (PNegLit (LitInt 1 TInteger)))")
--   </pre>
prettyPattern :: Pattern -> Doc ann
prettyType :: Type -> Doc ann
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Decl
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Expr
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Module
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Name
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Pattern
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Type
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty Aihc.Parser.Syntax.UnqualifiedName


-- | This module exposes the stable token-facing surface of the parser
--   without making callers depend on the internal lexer implementation. It
--   is intended for diagnostics, tooling, generators, and tests that need
--   to inspect the token stream directly instead of parsing it into the
--   AST.
--   
--   A <a>LexToken</a> records the token <a>LexTokenKind</a>, original
--   source text, source span, whether it started at the beginning of a
--   logical line, and its <a>TokenOrigin</a>. Tokens with
--   <a>FromSource</a> come from the input text; tokens with
--   <a>InsertedLayout</a> are virtual braces or semicolons inserted by the
--   layout pass.
--   
--   The <tt>lexTokens*</tt> functions scan arbitrary source fragments. The
--   <tt>lexModuleTokens*</tt> variants additionally read the module header
--   first, apply any <tt>LANGUAGE</tt> pragmas (including implied
--   extensions), and then run layout in module mode so top-level
--   declarations receive the same virtual layout tokens as the parser. Use
--   the <tt>WithSourceName</tt> variants when diagnostics should carry a
--   caller-provided source name instead of <tt>"<a>input</a>"</tt>.
--   
--   <a>readModuleHeaderExtensions</a> returns the raw extension settings
--   discovered in the leading module pragmas, while
--   <a>readModuleHeaderPragmas</a> separates a language edition from
--   explicit extension toggles.
module Aihc.Parser.Token
data TokenOrigin
FromSource :: TokenOrigin
InsertedLayout :: TokenOrigin
data LexToken
LexToken :: !LexTokenKind -> !Text -> !SourceSpan -> !TokenOrigin -> !Bool -> LexToken
[lexTokenKind] :: LexToken -> !LexTokenKind
[lexTokenText] :: LexToken -> !Text
[lexTokenSpan] :: LexToken -> !SourceSpan
[lexTokenOrigin] :: LexToken -> !TokenOrigin
[lexTokenAtLineStart] :: LexToken -> !Bool
data LexTokenKind
TkKeywordCase :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordClass :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordData :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordDefault :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordDeriving :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordDo :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordElse :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordForall :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordForeign :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordIf :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordImport :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordIn :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordInfix :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordInfixl :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordInfixr :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordInstance :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordLet :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordModule :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordNewtype :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordOf :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordThen :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordType :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordWhere :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordUnderscore :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordProc :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordRec :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordMdo :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordPattern :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordBy :: LexTokenKind
TkKeywordUsing :: LexTokenKind

-- | <tt>M.do</tt> — carries the module qualifier
TkQualifiedDo :: Text -> LexTokenKind

-- | <tt>M.mdo</tt> — carries the module qualifier
TkQualifiedMdo :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkReservedDotDot :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedColon :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedDoubleColon :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedEquals :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedBackslash :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedPipe :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedLeftArrow :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedRightArrow :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedAt :: LexTokenKind
TkReservedDoubleArrow :: LexTokenKind
TkPrefixPercent :: LexTokenKind
TkLinearArrow :: LexTokenKind
TkArrowTail :: LexTokenKind
TkArrowTailReverse :: LexTokenKind
TkDoubleArrowTail :: LexTokenKind
TkDoubleArrowTailReverse :: LexTokenKind
TkBananaOpen :: LexTokenKind
TkBananaClose :: LexTokenKind
TkVarId :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkConId :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkQVarId :: Text -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkQConId :: Text -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkImplicitParam :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkVarSym :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkConSym :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkQVarSym :: Text -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkQConSym :: Text -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkInteger :: Integer -> NumericType -> LexTokenKind
TkFloat :: Rational -> FloatType -> LexTokenKind
TkChar :: Char -> LexTokenKind
TkCharHash :: Char -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkString :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkStringHash :: Text -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkOverloadedLabel :: Text -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkSpecialLParen :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialRParen :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialUnboxedLParen :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialUnboxedRParen :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialComma :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialSemicolon :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialLBracket :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialRBracket :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialBacktick :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialLBrace :: LexTokenKind
TkSpecialRBrace :: LexTokenKind
TkMinusOperator :: LexTokenKind
TkPrefixMinus :: LexTokenKind
TkPrefixBang :: LexTokenKind
TkPrefixTilde :: LexTokenKind
TkRecordDot :: LexTokenKind
TkTypeApp :: LexTokenKind
TkPragma :: Pragma -> LexTokenKind
TkTHExpQuoteOpen :: LexTokenKind
TkTHExpQuoteClose :: LexTokenKind
TkTHTypedQuoteOpen :: LexTokenKind
TkTHTypedQuoteClose :: LexTokenKind
TkTHDeclQuoteOpen :: LexTokenKind
TkTHTypeQuoteOpen :: LexTokenKind
TkTHPatQuoteOpen :: LexTokenKind
TkTHQuoteTick :: LexTokenKind
TkTHTypeQuoteTick :: LexTokenKind
TkTHSplice :: LexTokenKind
TkTHTypedSplice :: LexTokenKind
TkQuasiQuote :: Text -> Text -> LexTokenKind
TkLineComment :: LexTokenKind
TkBlockComment :: LexTokenKind
TkError :: Text -> LexTokenKind
TkEOF :: LexTokenKind
readModuleHeaderExtensions :: Text -> [ExtensionSetting]
readModuleHeaderPragmas :: Text -> ModuleHeaderPragmas
lexTokensWithExtensions :: [Extension] -> Text -> [LexToken]
lexModuleTokensWithExtensions :: [Extension] -> Text -> [LexToken]
lexTokensWithSourceNameAndExtensions :: FilePath -> [Extension] -> Text -> [LexToken]
lexModuleTokensWithSourceNameAndExtensions :: FilePath -> [Extension] -> Text -> [LexToken]


-- | This module provides a compact, human-readable representation of
--   parsed AST structures via the <a>Shorthand</a> typeclass. Key
--   features:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Source spans are omitted to reduce noise</li>
--   <li>Empty fields (Nothing, [], False, etc.) and field labels are
--   omitted</li>
--   <li>Remaining constructor/value tokens are a subsequence of the
--   derived <a>Show</a> output</li>
--   <li>Output is on a single line by default</li>
--   <li>Uses the prettyprinter library for consistent formatting</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ snd $ parseModule defaultConfig "module Demo where x = 1"
--   Module {ModuleHead {"Demo"}, [DeclValue (PatternBind (PVar "x") (EInt 1 TInteger))]}
--   </pre>
module Aihc.Parser.Shorthand

-- | Typeclass for compact, human-readable AST representations.
--   
--   The <a>shorthand</a> method produces a <a>Doc</a> that can be rendered
--   to text or shown as a string. This is useful for debugging and golden
--   tests.
--   
--   Use <a>show</a> on the result of <a>shorthand</a> to get a
--   <a>String</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   show (shorthand expr) :: String
--   </pre>
class Shorthand a
shorthand :: Shorthand a => a -> Doc ()
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Decl
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Expr
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand Aihc.Parser.Lex.Types.LexToken
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand Aihc.Parser.Lex.Types.LexTokenKind
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Module
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand a => Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand (Aihc.Parser.Types.ParseResult a)
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Pattern
instance Aihc.Parser.Shorthand.Shorthand Aihc.Parser.Syntax.Type


-- | This module provides parsing functions for Haskell source code. The
--   main entry point is <a>parseModule</a> for parsing complete Haskell
--   modules. Additional functions are provided for parsing individual
--   expressions, patterns, and types.
module Aihc.Parser

-- | Parse a complete Haskell module.
--   
--   Returns any recovered parse errors alongside a (possibly partial)
--   <a>Module</a>. When individual declarations fail to parse, the parser
--   recovers and continues, returning the error and the successfully
--   parsed declarations.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ snd $ parseModule defaultConfig "module Main where\nmain = putStrLn \"Hello\""
--   Module {ModuleHead {"Main"}, [DeclValue (PatternBind (PVar "main") (EApp (EVar "putStrLn") (EString "Hello")))]}
--   </pre>
--   
--   Modules without a header are also supported:
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; case parseModule defaultConfig "x = 1" of { (_, m) -&gt; moduleName m }
--   Nothing
--   </pre>
parseModule :: ParserConfig -> Text -> ([(SourceSpan, Text)], Module)
data ParserConfig
ParserConfig :: FilePath -> [Extension] -> ParserConfig
[parserSourceName] :: ParserConfig -> FilePath
[parserExtensions] :: ParserConfig -> [Extension]

-- | Default parser configuration.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>parserSourceName</a> is set to <tt>"&lt;input&gt;"</tt></li>
--   <li><a>parserExtensions</a> is empty (no extensions enabled by
--   default)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; parserSourceName defaultConfig
--   "&lt;input&gt;"
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; parserExtensions defaultConfig
--   []
--   </pre>
defaultConfig :: ParserConfig
data ParseResult a
ParseOk :: a -> ParseResult a
ParseErr :: [(SourceSpan, Text)] -> ParseResult a

-- | Pretty-print a list of spanned parse errors with source context.
formatParseErrors :: FilePath -> Maybe Text -> [(SourceSpan, Text)] -> String

-- | Parse a Haskell expression.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parseExpr defaultConfig "1 + 2"
--   ParseOk (EInfix (EInt 1 TInteger) "+" (EInt 2 TInteger))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parseExpr defaultConfig "\\x -&gt; x + 1"
--   ParseOk (ELambdaPats [PVar "x"] (EInfix (EVar "x") "+" (EInt 1 TInteger)))
--   </pre>
--   
--   Parse errors are returned as <a>ParseErr</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; case parseExpr defaultConfig "1 +" of { ParseErr _ -&gt; "error"; ParseOk _ -&gt; "ok" }
--   "error"
--   </pre>
parseExpr :: ParserConfig -> Text -> ParseResult Expr

-- | Parse a Haskell signature type.
--   
--   This is the context used by top-level type signatures. Unlike
--   <a>parseType</a>, it rejects an unparenthesized outer kind signature:
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; case parseSignatureType defaultConfig "_ :: _" of { ParseErr _ -&gt; "error"; ParseOk _ -&gt; "ok" }
--   "error"
--   </pre>
parseSignatureType :: ParserConfig -> Text -> ParseResult Type

-- | Parse a Haskell type in the general declaration RHS context.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parseType defaultConfig "Int -&gt; Bool"
--   ParseOk (TFun (TCon "Int") (TCon "Bool"))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parseType defaultConfig "Maybe a"
--   ParseOk (TApp (TCon "Maybe") (TVar "a"))
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parseType defaultConfig "_ :: _"
--   ParseOk (TKindSig (TWildcard) (TWildcard))
--   </pre>
parseType :: ParserConfig -> Text -> ParseResult Type

-- | Parse a Haskell pattern.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parsePattern defaultConfig "(x, y)"
--   ParseOk (PTuple [PVar "x", PVar "y"])
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parsePattern defaultConfig "Just x"
--   ParseOk (PCon "Just" [PVar "x"])
--   </pre>
parsePattern :: ParserConfig -> Text -> ParseResult Pattern

-- | Parse a single Haskell declaration.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; shorthand $ parseDecl defaultConfig "f x = x + 1"
--   ParseOk (DeclValue (FunctionBind "f" [Match {MatchHeadPrefix, [PVar "x"], EInfix (EVar "x") "+" (EInt 1 TInteger)}]))
--   </pre>
parseDecl :: ParserConfig -> Text -> ParseResult Decl
